reason: Both are subject-area recent-paper listing pages (/list/<subject>/recent) with identical structure: 50 rows each, dt=50/dd=50 tag signature.
Replay is a category book-grid page; same article/h3/img/button counts scale proportionally to row count (11 vs 20), same page kind as the all-products seed.
Replay is a single-book detail page (/catalogue/tipping-the-velvet_999/) with rows=1, tr/th/td product-spec table, and no article grid.
Replay is a user profile page (/user?id=pg, title 'Profile: pg') with rows=1 and a collapsed DOM; not a story listing.
Replay is a tag-filtered story listing (/t/programming) with 25 rows and a nearly identical tag shape (img=25, time=25, details=20) — same kind as the frontpage seed.
Replay is a PyPI search results page for a different query (?q=http) with 20 rows and bit-for-bit identical tag counts — same page kind.
Replay is a single package detail page (/project/Flask/) with rows=0 and a rich-prose DOM (p=156, code=42, strong=34) — not a search results listing.
Replay is an author biography page (/author/Albert-Einstein/) with rows=0 and a tiny DOM (7 divs, 5 p, 1 h1); not a quotes listing.
Replay is a subreddit listing page (body_class=listing-page hot-page, 25 rows) with a tag shape proportionally identical to the seed — different subreddit/sort but same page kind.
Replay is an EDGAR filing-search results page for a different company (CIK 0000789019) with title 'EDGAR Search Results', 33 rows, and near-identical tag shape — same kind.
Replay is the SEC.gov homepage (title 'SEC.gov | Home', body_class=path-frontpage, rows=0) — a portal/marketing page completely unlike an EDGAR search results table.
Replay is a single Q&A detail page (body_class=question-page, rows=0) with a massive answer/code DOM (code=460, pre=90, p=323) — not a questions listing.
Replay is another product-category grid (/phones/touch, 9 rows) with the same tag signature (div/span/h4/img/meta scaling with row count) — same page kind as the laptops seed.

Replay is another Wikipedia Category page (ns-14 in body_class, similar bdi/h3 tag shape); both are category-member listings, though the seed's rows=1 and replay's rows=0 suggest the row selector is poorly calibrated on these pages.
Replay is a Wikipedia article (ns-0 namespace, title 'Cinema of the United States') with rows=0 and a citation-heavy DOM (sup=284, cite=163) — not a category member listing.
Replay is a Wikipedia article page (ns-0, title '映画') with rows=0 and a citation/sup-heavy DOM (sup=65, cite=27) — not a category listing page.
Replay is another Wiktionary category listing (Category:English_verbs) with 200 rows and a near-identical tag shape — same page kind as the English_lemmas seed.
